title: Microfluidic check valve
abstract: Microfluidic check valve (1), including a housing (10) with at least one closed channel (12), wherein the closed channel comprises a first sector (14) and a second sector (16), wherein the cross-sectional area (b) of the second sector (16) is larger than the cross-sectional area (a) of the first sector (14), wherein a sheet (20) is arranged in the second sector (16), wherein said sheet (20) is anchored in a channel inlet (22) ending in the second sector (16), said sheet (20) further consisting of a material that allows the sheet (20) to hinge between an open position and a closed position if pressure is applied by a fluid stream onto said sheet (20), and said sheet (20) tightly covering the cross-sectional area (a) of the first sector (14) if said sheet (20) is in the closed position.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Microfluidic check valve (1), including a housing (10) with at least one closed channel (12), wherein the closed channel comprises a first sector (14) and a second sector (16), wherein the cross-sectional area (b) of the second sector (16) is larger than the cross- sectional area (a) of the first sector (14), wherein a sheet (20) is arranged in the second sector (16), wherein said sheet (20) is anchored in a channel inlet (22) ending in the second sector (16), said sheet (20) further consisting of a material that allows the sheet (20) to hinge between an open position and a closed position if pressure is applied by a fluid stream onto said sheet (20), and said sheet (20) tightly covering the cross-sectional area (a) of the first sector (14) if said sheet (20) is in the closed position. 2. Microfluidic check valve according to claim 1, wherein the sheet (20) is directly anchored at the end of the channel inlet (22) ending in the second sector (16). 3. Microfluidic check valve according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein said sheet (20) consists of a polymer material that allows the sheet (20) passively to hinge between an open position and a closed position if pressure is applied by a fluid stream onto said sheet (20). 4. Microfluidic check valve according to one of claims 1 to 3, wherein said sheet (20) consists of a polymerized material, wherein the channel inlet (22) ends in the second sector (16) and takes up a portion of the sheet (20). 5. Microfluidic check valve according to one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the sheet (20) consists of a polymer and wherein the channel inlet (22) comprises a bottleneck and the sheet is polymerized before and after the bottleneck to form an anchor for the sheet (20). 6. Microfluidic check valve according to one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the housing (10) is at least partially translucent. 7. Microfluidic check valve according to one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the sheet (20) is a photopolymerisable polymer. 8. Microfluidic check valve according to one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the second sector (16) is essentially cylindrical. 9. Microfluidic check valve according to one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the sheet (20) has a shape and size that extends the cross section (a) of the first sector (14) at the position where it joins the second sector (16). 10. Microfluidic check valve according to one of claims 1 to 9, characterised by a reinforcing mounting element (24) in the second sector (16) in proximity to the channel inlet (22). 11. Bio-micro-electro-mechanical-system, micro/Nanofluidic-System, Lab on a Chip- System, or Total Analysis System, comprising a microfluidic check valve according to any of claims 1 to 10. 12. System according to claim 11, selected from the group consisting of drug delivery apparatus, health monitoring apparatus, diagnosis apparatus, DNA analysis apparatus, blood analyzing apparatus, treatment apparatus, preferably blood treatment apparatus, diagnostic device. 13. A method of preparing a microfluidic check valve (1), comprising providing the housing (10) with at least one closed channel (12), wherein the closed channel (12) comprises a first sector (14) and a second sector (16), wherein the cross-sectional area (b) of the second sector (16) is larger than the cross- sectional area (a) of the first sector (14), further providing a channel inlet (22) ending in the second sector (14), wherein a polymerisable monomer is filled via said channel inlet (22) arranged in the second sector (14) so as to at least partially fill the second sector (14), wherein said polymerisable monomer is exposed to a radiation source to polymerise the monomer such that a sheet (20) is formed that may hinge between an open position and a closed position if pressure is applied by a fluid stream on said sheet (20), and such that the sheet (20) is anchored in the channel inlet (22). 14. A method according to claim 13, wherein said polymerisable monomer is a photopolymerisable monomer and wherein said radiation source is light. 15. Microfluidic pump (50), comprising a first microfluidic check valve (1) according to one of claims 1 to 10 and a second microfluidic check valve ( ) according to one of claims 1 to 10 in a serial arrangement further comprising a controllable element for increasing pressure 

 (52) arranged between said first microfluidic check valve (1) and said second microfluidic check valve ( ). 16. Microfluidic pump according to claim 15, wherein the controllable element for increasing pressure is a heating element (52). 17. Microfluidic pump according to claim 15 or claim 16, characterised by an array of microfluidic check valves (1) according to one of claims 1 to 10 with at least one a controllable element for increasing pressure (52) arranged between each pair of microfluidic check valves (1, ). 18. Microfluidic check valve array (60), comprising at least two microfluidic check valves (1) according to one of claims 1 to 10, with each microfluidic check valve (1) comprising an input channel (62), an exit channel and the microfluidic check valve (1) being arranged between input (62) und exit channel, wherein the exit channels end up in a chamber (66), wherein the chamber (66) is connected to an output channel (68).
